{"step_by_step_thinking": "Enterobius vermicularis, Hymenolepis nana, and Taenia solium are known to have the capability of autoinfection. Enterobius vermicularis (pinworm) can reinfect itself through the perianal area. Hymenolepis nana (dwarf tapeworm) can autoinfect via the ingestion of its own eggs. Taenia solium (pork tapeworm) can autoinfect through the ingestion of its own eggs. However, Ascaris lumbricoides (roundworm) does not have the capability of autoinfection as it does not multiply in the host and requires reinfection through the ingestion of contaminated feces.", "answer_choice": "D"}
